text: is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。

text: Mulch is material spread over soil. soil.
zh: [1] 覆盖物是铺在土壤上的材料。

text: It protects against wind and rain damage.
zh: [2] 它可以防止风雨对土壤的损害。

text: It can also limit weed growth. Finding materials for organic mulch can be easy.
zh: [3] 它还能抑制杂草生长。寻找有机覆盖材料并不难。

text: Small pieces of tree bark or cut -up leaves can be used.
zh: [4] 小块树皮或切碎的树叶都可以使用。

text: Some people use grass cuttings or newspapers.
zh: [5] 有些人使用剪下的草屑或旧报纸。

text: Others use inorganic material such as shredded rubber from tires.
zh: [6] 也有人使用无机材料,比如轮胎碎胶。

text: Mulch is important for home gardens but also to farms.
zh: [7] 覆盖物不仅对家庭花园重要,对农场也同样重要。

text: Farmers may keep the remains of maize or other crops on top of the soil.
zh: [8] 农民可能会把玉米或其他作物的残余物留在土壤表层。

text: The crop waste creates mulch. This practice is called conservation tillage.
zh: [9] 这些作物残余就形成了覆盖层。这种做法被称为保护性耕作。

text: Organic mulch protects but also improves the condition of the soil.
zh: [10] 有机覆盖物不仅能保护土壤,还能改善土壤状况。

text: It provides nutrients for plants. It also provides a good environment
zh: [11] 它为植物提供养分,同时也为蚯蚓和其他有益的土壤生物

text: for earthworms and other helpful organisms in the soil.
zh: [12] 提供了良好的生存环境。

text: As the mulch breaks down, it provides material that keeps the soil from getting hard.
zh: [13] 随着覆盖物逐渐分解,它会提供物质,防止土壤板结。

text: This improves the growth of roots and increases the movement of water through the soil.
zh: [14] 这有助于根系生长,并增加水分在土壤中的流动。

text: The mulch also improves the ability of the soil to hold moisture.
zh: [15] 覆盖物还能提高土壤的保湿能力。

text: Experts generally suggest laying mulch to a depth of about 10 centimeters.
zh: [16] 专家通常建议覆盖层厚度约为10厘米。

text: If finer materials are used, such as cut grass,
zh: [17] 如果使用较细的材料,比如剪下的草屑,

text: the depth should be 2 .5 to 7 .5 centimeters.
The same is true if compost is used for mulch.
zh: [19] 如果使用堆肥作为覆盖物,情况也是如此。

text: Too much mulch can restrict oxygen and water flow to the soil and harm roots.
zh: [20] 覆盖物过厚会限制土壤中氧气和水分的流通,损害根系。

text: Also, do not build up mulch around the base of trees or plants like a volcano.
zh: [21] 另外,不要把覆盖物像火山一样堆积在树木或植物的根部周围。

text: This could lead to disease or insect damage.
zh: [22] 这可能导致病害或虫害。

text: Some gardeners use leaf mulch, fine particles of leaves,
zh: [23] 有些园丁使用叶覆盖物,也就是细碎的树叶,

text: instead of wood mulch around trees, especially young trees.
zh: [24] 来代替木质覆盖物铺在树木周围,尤其是幼树。

text: Leaf mulch can also be used in flower beds.
zh: [25] 叶覆盖物也可以用在花坛里。

text: Mulching is one of the best things people can do for their gardens.
zh: [26] 覆盖是人们为花园所能做的最好的事情之一。

text: It helps keep the soil from getting dry, so it reduces the need for watering.
zh: [27] 它有助于保持土壤湿润,从而减少浇水的需要。

text: It also limits temperature changes in the soil.
zh: [28] 它还能限制土壤温度的变化。

text: Mulch forms a barrier between the soil and the air.
zh: [29] 覆盖物在土壤和空气之间形成了一道屏障。

text: As a result, mulched soil will be cooler in the summertime, time,
zh: [30] 因此,覆盖过的土壤在夏天会更凉爽,

text: and in winter it may not freeze as deeply as areas without mulch.
zh: [31] 而在冬天,其冻结深度也不会像没有覆盖的地方那么深。

text: can be added late in autumn to help moderate the effects of winter weather.
zh: [32] 可以在深秋时添加覆盖物,以缓解冬季天气的影响。

text: The best time is after the ground has frozen, but before the coldest weather arrives.
zh: [33] 最佳时间是在地面结冰之后,但在最寒冷的天气到来之前。

text: Spreading mulch before the ground has frozen
zh: [34] 如果在地面结冰之前就铺上覆盖物,

text: may attract small animals searching for a warm place to spend the winter.
zh: [35] 可能会吸引一些正在寻找过冬温暖场所的小动物。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [36] 以上就是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。

text: Archives of our reports, including last week's explanation of compost,
zh: [37] 我们节目的存档,包括上周关于堆肥的讲解,

text: can be found at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [38] 都可以在voaspecialenglish.com网站上找到。
